subject: HIST
course_description: Through a series of thematic units, case studies, and analytical writing assignments, this course examines the emergence and evolution of modern terrorism in the United States and the impact of international terrorism on American society and foreign policy.
course_notes: (Same as AMST 385 1) (Terrorism is often portrayed as a new and unprecedented evil that arrived in the United States at the dawn of the 21st century. But the origins of terrorism, and its impact on American history, reach back much further. Terrorism - as a philosophy, tactic, and security threat - has played a significant role in the history of the United States and its role in the world. This course focuses on the origins of modern terrorism and its impact on American politics, culture, and foreign policy. The course it begins by establishing a working definition of terrorism that we will revisit and reevaluate throughout the semester. Through a series thematic units and case studies, students will examine how terrorist ideology and tactics have or have not changed over time. Through discussions, readings, and films, students will analyze why various groups justified and employed terrorism. Students will also evaluate how government officials and everyday people responded to terrorism.)
